H1_same_entity_deposit_withdrawal.name: same_entity_deposit_withdrawal
H1_same_entity_deposit_withdrawal.description: Deposit and withdrawal transactions linked to the same entity or wallet.

signals.name: same_entity_deposit_withdrawal_same_cex
signals.description: Deposit and withdrawal transactions are linked through the same exchange account.

signals.name: same_entity_deposit_withdrawal_ens_name
signals.description: The withdrawal address is an ENS name controlled by the depositor.

signals.name: same_entity_deposit_withdrawal_previous_transaction_source
signals.description: The withdrawal address has received tokens from the same source as the deposit address.

signals.name: same_entity_deposit_withdrawal_fresh_address
signals.description: The withdrawal is made to a completely new address with no prior transaction history.

recommendations.description: Use different exchange accounts for deposits and withdrawals to avoid linking them.
Do not use ENS names controlled by the depositor for withdrawal addresses to avoid clear linking.
Regularly monitor and verify funding sources of addresses involved in transactions to prevent misuse.
Utilize relay services or anonymizing mixers to obscure the direct link between deposit and withdrawal addresses.

H2_clean_address_behavior.name: clean_address_behavior
H2_clean_address_behavior.description: Withdrawals to fresh, unlinked addresses with no prior history, indicating privacy measures.

signals.name: clean_address_withdrawals
signals.description: Withdrawals made from stealth addresses directly to new, unlinked addresses that have no prior transaction history.

signals.name: ens_redirection
signals.description: Withdrawals from stealth address to ENS names where the name's owner matches or is linked to the depositor.

signals.name: same_source_token_flow
signals.description: Identifying withdrawal addresses that have previously received tokens from the same source as the deposit address, suggesting reuse of an old address.

signals.name: cross_cex_deposit
signals.description: Withdrawals sent to a different CEX deposit address than the original source, indicating an attempt to obscure transaction history.

recommendations.description: Generate new addresses for each transaction to avoid linking behavior.
Avoid using ENS names when possible, as they may reveal user identity.
Deposit and withdraw funds through multiple exchanges to break the chain of custody.
Use mixing services that add additional obfuscation layers to transactions.

H3_gas_pattern_consistency.name: gas_pattern_consistency
H3_gas_pattern_consistency.description: Repeated use of identical or consistent gas pricing strategies across multiple transactions.

signals.name: consistent_gas_pricing
signals.description: Transactions use the exact same gas price in multiple operations, suggesting a consistent strategy.

signals.name: abrupt_gas_increase
signals.description: Sudden increase in gas price after initial transactions with low gas costs, indicating potential detection of stealth address.

signals.name: cross_platform_gas_consistency
signals.description: Transactions across different platforms or wallets use the same consistent gas pricing strategy, indicating a coordinated effort to maintain anonymity.

signals.name: withdrawal_to_known_source
signals.description: Withdrawals are sent back to addresses that have previously interacted with the same source as the deposit address, suggesting an attempt to reuse or trace funds.

recommendations.description: Use a randomized gas pricing strategy to avoid detection through consistent patterns.
Integrate with decentralized price oracles for dynamic gas pricing, reducing the predictability of transactions.
Automatically monitor for any sudden changes in gas pricing strategy to detect potential stealth address deanonymization attempts.
Avoid using the same gas pricing strategy across different platforms or wallets to break up any consistent patterns that could be used for deanonymization.
Utilize mixing services or tumbler tools to make withdrawal patterns less traceable back to the source address.

H4_immediate_spend_behavior.name: immediate_spend_behavior
H4_immediate_spend_behavior.description: Deposits followed immediately by withdrawals, reducing the anonymity set.

signals.name: immediate_deposit_withdrawal
signals.description: A stealth address receives a deposit and immediately sends funds out, likely to the same or closely associated entity.

signals.name: withdraw_to_known_entity
signals.description: Funds are withdrawn from a stealth address and sent to an address known to belong to the same entity as the deposit source, indicating a low anonymity set.

signals.name: fresh_address_behavior
signals.description: Funds are withdrawn from a stealth address to an entirely new and previously unspent address, suggesting an attempt at obfuscation but potentially revealing the real identity if correlated.

signals.name: cross_exchange_behavior
signals.description: A withdrawal from a stealth address goes to an exchange deposit address, possibly indicating funds are being moved between exchanges or back to the original source.

recommendations.description: Utilize mixing services to increase the anonymity set by breaking the direct link between deposit and withdrawal.
Delay withdrawals after receiving deposits to avoid immediate spend behavior that reduces anonymity set.
Distribute funds across multiple exchanges to complicate cross-exchange transaction patterns.
Create unique intermediate addresses for each deposit and withdrawal to obfuscate the direct relationship.
Identify known entity interactions early and adjust transaction strategy to avoid predictable patterns.

H5_direct_funding.name: direct_funding
H5_direct_funding.description: Gas costs funded directly from the depositor's main wallet or known entities.

signals.name: direct_gas_funding
signals.description: A transaction sending small amounts (usually under 0.5 ETH) to stealth addresses is detected from known depositor wallets.

signals.name: consistent_gas_sender
signals.description: Multiple transactions of small amounts sent from the same wallet address to various stealth addresses.

signals.name: known_entity_funding
signals.description: Transactions for small amounts from known entities (previously linked to depositor) directly funding stealth addresses.

signals.name: recent_gas_transactions
signals.description: Recent history of a wallet showing frequent small transactions (under 1 ETH) to stealth addresses.

recommendations.description: Implement multi-signature wallets for transactions to increase security.
Increase the amount of gas used in transactions to avoid detection by heuristic rules focusing on small amounts.
Utilize mixer services that shuffle transaction inputs and outputs to obscure funding origins.
Regularly rotate the use of wallet addresses for transactions to reduce the chance of pattern recognition.
Mask the entity relationship data when transferring small amounts to stealth addresses.

H6_self_send_activity.name: self_send_activity
H6_self_send_activity.description: Transfer of funds between addresses controlled by the same entity, reducing anonymity set.

signals.name: self_send_deposit_withdrawal
signals.description: A user deposits funds into a stealth address and then withdraws those funds to another address controlled by the same entity.

signals.name: self_send_ens_name
signals.description: A user deposits funds into a stealth address and then withdraws to an ENS name known to be owned by the depositor.

signals.name: self_send_token_flow
signals.description: A user deposits tokens into a stealth address and withdraws them to an address that has previously received funds from the same source.

signals.name: self_send_fresh_address
signals.description: A user withdraws funds to an address with no prior transaction history, indicating it may be controlled by the same entity as the stealth address.

signals.name: self_send_cex_deposit
signals.description: A user withdraws funds to a different CEX deposit address, suggesting the same entity controls both addresses.

recommendations.description: Use unique addresses for each transaction to avoid patterns that could link stealth addresses.
Enable built-in anonymity features in the wallet software, such as enhanced privacy modes that randomize transaction sequences.
Use Tor or similar anonymizing networks when accessing wallet services to mask IP addresses and reduce deanonymization risks.
Deploy a monitoring tool that alerts when funds are moved between likely related addresses, allowing immediate response to mitigate exposure.
Implement advanced mixing services that anonymize transactions through a complex web of transfers, reducing the ability to trace funds.
Avoid depositing and withdrawing between stealth addresses and centralized exchange accounts, reducing detectable patterns.

H7_unique_transaction_value.name: unique_transaction_value
H7_unique_transaction_value.description: Deposits or transactions with unique or rare amounts that are easily identifiable.

signals.name: unique_deposit_amount
signals.description: Identifies transactions where the amount is unique or rare within the network, making it traceable back to the original depositor.

signals.name: correlated_withens
signals.description: Detects a withdrawal to an ENS name that is owned by the same entity as the stealth address depositor.

signals.name: history_less_address
signals.description: Identifies a withdrawal to an address with no prior history, suggesting it was created specifically for this transaction.

signals.name: cross_exchange_deposit
signals.description: Recognizes withdrawals sent back to a different CEX deposit address, indicating the funds were likely reintegrated into another exchange account.

recommendations.description: Use standardized transaction amounts that are common within the network to blend in.
Implement an automated tool to randomly generate transaction amounts close to intended values, but varied enough to avoid uniqueness.
Do not use ENS names owned by the same entity for deposits or withdrawals to prevent correlation.
Create temporary wallet addresses specifically for each transaction to reduce the risk of history-based detection.
Avoid sending funds back to different CEX deposit addresses from where they were withdrawn; use direct withdrawal methods instead.
Utilize advanced mixing services that obscure transaction amounts and patterns more effectively.
